SEARCH
Package uk.ac.leedsbeckett.oop

Class GameWorldException

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Throwable
java.lang.Exception
java.lang.RuntimeException
uk.ac.leedsbeckett.oop.OOPrailwaySimException
uk.ac.leedsbeckett.oop.GameWorldException
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class GameWorldException extends OOPrailwaySimException
Exception thrown when an invalid operation is attempted on the game world. This is a runtime exception used to signal errors related to game world state and operations.

GameWorldException is thrown when:

  • Attempting to toggle a non-existent crossing
  • Attempting to place a vehicle on a position with no track
  • Attempting to access or modify invalid positions in the world grid
  • Attempting operations on world elements that don't exist
  • Invalid world configuration or state

Why RuntimeException? This extends OOPrailwaySimException (which extends RuntimeException) because game world errors typically represent programming bugs, invalid configurations, or incorrect usage of the API that should not normally occur during correct operation. They don't need to be caught and handled in normal code flow.

Example Usage:

 public void toggleCrossing(int index) {
     if (index < 0 || index >= crossings.size()) {
         throw new GameWorldException("Invalid crossing index: " + index + 
                                     ". Valid range: 0-" + (crossings.size() - 1));
     }
     crossings.get(index).toggle();
 }

Comparison with other exceptions:

  • GameWorldException: For general world state and configuration errors
  • MovementException: Specifically for vehicle movement and derailment issues
  • LayoutException: For layout file parsing and validation errors
See Also:
  • MovementException
  • LayoutException
  • OOPrailwaySimException
  • GameWorld
  • Vehicle
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    GameWorldException(String message)
    Creates a new GameWorldException with the specified error message.
    GameWorldException(String message, Throwable cause)
    Creates a new GameWorldException with the specified error message and cause.

  • Method Summary

  • Constructor Details

    • GameWorldException

      public GameWorldException(String message)
      Creates a new GameWorldException with the specified error message. The message should clearly describe what world operation failed and why.
      Parameters:
      message - a description of the game world error that occurred

    • GameWorldException

      public GameWorldException(String message, Throwable cause)
      Creates a new GameWorldException with the specified error message and cause. Use this when wrapping another exception that caused the game world error.
      Parameters:
      message - a description of the game world error that occurred
      cause - the underlying exception that caused this error
